title: Lesson Quiz: Demand and Consumer Behavior
description: Assessment of the concept that Law of demand, consumer choices, and demand shifts.

questions.prompt: What does the law of demand state?

choices.text: As price increases, quantity demanded decreases
choices.feedback: Correct. The law of demand states that higher prices usually lead to lower quantity demanded.

choices.text: As price increases, quantity demanded increases
choices.feedback: This is incorrect. Demand and price usually move in opposite directions.
This answer refers to supply, not demand.

choices.text: Demand never changes when price changes
choices.feedback: This is incorrect. Price changes affect quantity demanded.

questions.prompt: A movement along the demand curve is caused by a change in the price of the product itself.

choices.feedback: Correct. A change in the product's own price causes movement along the demand curve.
This is incorrect. A price change causes movement along the demand curve, not a shift.

questions.prompt: Which of the following would most likely shift demand rather than cause movement along the demand curve?

choices.text: A change in the price of the good itself
choices.feedback: A change in the price of the good itself causes movement along the demand curve.
Correct. Income is a determinant of demand and can shift the entire demand curve.

choices.text: A lower price for the same product
choices.feedback: A lower price changes quantity demanded, not demand itself.

choices.text: A higher price for the same product
choices.feedback: A higher price changes quantity demanded, not demand itself.

choices.text: A product that must be used together with another product
choices.feedback: That describes a complement, not a substitute.

choices.text: A product that consumers buy only when income falls
choices.feedback: This does not define a substitute good.

choices.text: A product that can be used in place of another product
choices.feedback: Correct. A substitute is an alternative product consumers can choose instead.

choices.text: A product produced by the same company
choices.feedback: Products do not have to come from the same company to be substitutes.

questions.prompt: A change in consumer preferences can shift the demand curve.

choices.feedback: Correct. Preferences are a determinant of demand and can shift the curve.
This is incorrect. Preferences do affect demand and can shift the demand curve.
